mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or already low, equity discount rates ease and risk appetite improves, so S&P 500 returns over the next month are positive. The signal fails if rate cuts coincide with acute growth shocks that dominate valuation support.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in a majority of low-or-falling FEDFUNDS observations, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above recent trend signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, which should pressure equities lower over the next month. The effect is strongest when the labor market is still worsening rather than merely high.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the sign of the effect is not negative in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in retail sales proxies weakening consumer demand and corporate revenue expectations, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next month. The mechanism fails when retail weakness is fully offset by simultaneous monetary easing.
failure_condition: Reject if negative RSAFS shocks do not lead to below-zero average 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears once FEDFUNDS is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: Job losses combined with rising unemployment identify a true labor-market deterioration, and the joint signal should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. Either series alone is weaker; the interaction captures broadening stress rather than noise.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform each single proxy, or if SPX returns are not negative when PAYEMS falls and UNRATE rises in the same monthly window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals falling real activity and weaker earnings momentum, which should depress equity prices over the following quarter. The effect should be clearer when production is below its recent local peak.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month production declines are not followed by negative average 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips positive in consecutive rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in housing starts indicates improving cyclical demand and easier financing conditions, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal fails if starts rise only during a late-cycle inflation shock.
failure_condition: Reject if positive HOUST momentum does not correspond to above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the relationship is absent after controlling for the recent level of FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent drop in CPI inflation and the federal funds rate signals successful disinflation and easier policy, which should lift equity multiples and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The interaction matters because lower inflation alone is insufficient if policy is still tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined low-inflation and falling-rate state does not outperform either series alone, or if triggered windows fail to show positive average 20-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary labor regime, a falling payroll series paired with rising unemployment indicates broad income stress and should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. This short-horizon signal is strongest when layoffs are still accelerating.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls and UNRATE rises in the same monthly release
failure_condition: Reject if same-month payroll declines plus unemployment increases do not produce negative average 5-day SPX returns, or if the signal is positive in most triggered instances.
